evals.query: In my Kora service I want to cache OrderDto lookups by their UUID id using a local in-memory cache. How do I set this up?
expected_behavior: Adds the ru.tinkoff.kora:cache-caffeine dependency and the mandatory annotation-processors (Java) or symbol-processors (Kotlin)
Connects CaffeineCacheModule on the @KoraApp interface
Declares a typed @Cache("...") interface extending CaffeineCache<UUID, OrderDto>
Annotates the read method with @Cacheable(OrderCache.class) so the single UUID argument becomes the cache key
Provides a HOCON config block with maximumSize and/or expireAfterWrite at the configured @Cache path
Does not use field injection or external-framework caching annotations (@EnableCaching); uses only Kora's @Cacheable

evals.query: I need to cache by a combination of tenantId and orderId in a Kora Caffeine cache. How do I model the composite key?
expected_behavior: Declares an inner record (Java) or data class (Kotlin) Key(tenantId, orderId) inside the @Cache interface
Extends CaffeineCache<OrderCache.Key, OrderDto> with the Key type
Notes that method argument order must match the record component order, or uses the parameters attribute to reorder
Keeps @Cacheable(OrderCache.class) on the method
Does not invent a non-existent condition or unless attribute on the cache annotations

evals.query: My cached method takes an OrderContext object (tenantId, orderId, requestId) but the cache key is just tenantId+orderId. How do I derive the key in Kora?
expected_behavior: Implements CacheKeyMapper from ru.tinkoff.kora.cache to map OrderContext to the cache Key type
Applies the mapper with @Mapping(OrderContextMapper.class) alongside @Cacheable
Uses CacheKeyMapper.CacheKeyMapper2 (a nested interface) when mapping from two separate method arguments rather than a single object
Does not use SpEL-style key expressions; derives the key only via CacheKeyMapper/@Mapping

evals.query: After updating and deleting an order I want to keep the Kora cache consistent. Which annotations do I use and how do I pick the key?
expected_behavior: Uses @CachePut on the update method so the method always runs and the returned value is written to the cache
Uses @CacheInvalidate on the delete method to evict the key after the method returns
Uses the parameters attribute (e.g. parameters = {"id"}) to select which arguments form the key when the value object is also a parameter
Mentions @CacheInvalidate(value = ..., invalidateAll = true) for clearing the whole cache
Does not use @CacheEvict (a different framework); uses Kora's @CacheInvalidate and @CachePut

evals.query: Set up a distributed Kora cache for OrderDto backed by Redis so several pods share cache state.
expected_behavior: Adds ru.tinkoff.kora:cache-redis and connects RedisCacheModule
Declares @Cache interface extending RedisCache<UUID, @Json OrderDto> with @Json (ru.tinkoff.kora.json.common.annotation.Json) on the value type for serialization
Configures the shared lettuce { uri = ... } driver section, externalizing the URI/credentials with env substitution
Sets the required keyPrefix on the per-cache config and explains the graph build fails without it
Does not use RedisTemplate or a raw Jedis/Lettuce client by hand; uses the typed RedisCache contract

evals.query: I want a two-level cache in Kora: a fast local Caffeine L1 in front of a shared Redis L2. How do I wire that?
expected_behavior: Connects both CaffeineCacheModule and RedisCacheModule on the @KoraApp interface
Declares two separate @Cache interfaces with distinct config paths: CaffeineCache for L1 and RedisCache<..., @Json ...> for L2
Stacks @Cacheable annotations on the method with the Caffeine (L1) annotation first and the Redis (L2) annotation second, since aspects apply top to bottom
Gives L1 a shorter TTL than L2 and notes the Redis cache still needs keyPrefix
Does not rely on a @Caching meta-annotation or field injection; uses constructor injection and stacked Kora annotations

evals.query: I need manual get-or-load behavior on a Kora cache without using the @Cacheable aspect, for example to warm the cache after generating an id. What are my options?
expected_behavior: Injects the typed cache component and calls the imperative Cache API: get(key), put(key, value), computeIfAbsent(key, loader), invalidate(key)
Notes get() returns null on a miss and the value must be put explicitly
Offers LoadableCache via cache.asLoadable(loader) as a get-or-load wrapper exposed as a @Root component
Does not claim @Cacheable can read a key produced inside the method body
Does not use a CacheManager abstraction or @Cacheable for this manual flow; uses the injected Cache directly

evals.query: I added @Cacheable to a method in my Kora service but the cache is never hit and the method always runs. What is wrong?
expected_behavior: Explains Kora cache annotations are applied via compile-time AOP through a generated subclass proxy, so the target must be non-final in Java or open in Kotlin
Checks that the annotation-processors (Java) / symbol-processors (KSP, Kotlin) dependency is present so the aspect is generated
Notes self-invocation bypass: calls from within the same component skip the proxy, so call through an injected component
Suggests inspecting the generated $Service__AopProxy to confirm the cache lookup was woven in
Does not suggest enabling runtime proxies/AspectJ or @EnableCaching; the fix is compile-time AOP plus a non-final/open target
